scope: Project-level reusable multi-state character reference board for Li Tang, the high-ranking lower-caste Suming combat insect commander.

locked_references.role: locked Suming/Qingming white-wing-over-black-sun emblem reference; do not redesign
locked Suming/Qingming symbol-extension reference for wax seals, plaques, carapace brands, and military tags
global low-magic eastern epic mood lock
project material and tactile surface lock
Qingming insect-wax, bone-white, membrane, and institutional material lock
border-wall blackstone, snowline, old iron, and frost material lock

downstream_usage: Hanwubao and border-wall combat continuity reference
Suming lower-caste combat insect hierarchy reference

batch_rules: Do not redesign existing faction emblems.
Use the locked Suming/Qingming white-wing-over-black-sun emblem only as a small physical plaque, wax seal, carapace brand, or military tag.

Li Tang must be clearly non-human and non-humanoid: forward-leaning combat insect body, triangular mantis head, dark compound eyes, mandible guard, broad hard carapace, reverse-jointed legs, and folding forearm blades.

Li Tang's commander face must be more anthropomorphic than ordinary insect soldiers: readable brow ridge, nose-like chitin ridge, cheek-plate expression planes, narrow jaw armor, and command anger/judgment, while retaining compound eyes, bone-white mandible guard, triangular mantis skull, antenna roots, carapace cheeks, and dark neck membrane.
Do not turn Li Tang into a human general, knight, armored warrior, martial-arts villain, or monster wearing a full robe.
Do not leave the face as a pure unreadable mantis monster head or rank-and-file bug soldier face; the semi-anthropomorphic command face is a required hierarchy marker.
Costume language is harness and military control gear: old iron bindings, dark-green command belt, bone-white insect-wax strips, black short mantle, short spear, and iron gate command token.
Distress surfaces through frost, old iron wear, blade scratches, wax cracks, and limited dark-red blade marks; avoid gore and monster-horror excess.
No visible text, labels, captions, UI, watermark, Chinese characters, or Latin letters inside generated images.
